label: 1589 CE
era_summary: 1589 (MDLXXXIX) was a common year starting on Sunday of the Gregorian calendar and a common year starting on Wednesday of the Julian calendar, the 1589th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 589th year of the 2nd millennium, the 89th year of the 16th century, and the 10th and last year of the 1580s decade. As of the start of 1589, the Gregorian calendar was 10 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Brecke records no major conflicts touching this region in 1589 (safety scored at the era baseline of 85). No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1589: Netherlands-Spain, 1566-1609; Cologne (with intervention by Spain, Palatinate and others), 1583-89; France (Huguenots, with intervention by Spain and England), 1585-98. Maddison records GDP/capita 1269 (ESP, 1589) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
